name: Electrical Transformer
tagline: AC magnetic coupling that steps voltage up for transmission and down for safe use — without moving parts
problem:
Transmitting megawatts at utilisation voltage would require absurd conductor cross-section; end equipment cannot insulate hundreds of kilovolts inside a home. The transformer solves impedance transformation for AC: trade voltage against current while conserving power minus losses.

Design challenges include core losses from hysteresis and eddy currents, copper losses from I²R, stray flux heating structural steel, and insulation ageing from partial discharge in paper-oil systems. Short-circuit forces between windings can crush coils if bracing is naive.

DC networks cannot use classical transformers — a fact that shaped the War of Currents and later motivated power electronics for HVDC taps.
overview:
Two or more windings link through a shared core flux. Primary ampere-turns drive flux limited by core permeability and air gaps; secondary voltage scales by turns ratio. Shell or core-type constructions route flux; three-phase banks use delta and wye connections for grounding and third-harmonic management.

Liquid-filled units use mineral or ester oil for cooling and insulation, with radiators or fans. Dry-type resin cast transformers suit indoor switchgear. Tap changers adjust ratio under load to regulate voltage as feeders stretch.

Protection includes differential relays comparing terminal currents, sudden pressure relays on tanks, and hot-spot temperature estimates from winding resistance models.

Transformer hum is magnetostriction at twice line frequency — the audible signature of cities.

principles.name: Faraday induction
principles.explanation: Time-varying flux induces voltage proportional to turns — the basis of all AC transformation.

principles.name: Ideal transformer relationships
principles.explanation: Voltage ratio equals turns ratio; current ratio inversely; impedance scales as squared turns ratio referred across.

principles.name: Per-unit system
principles.explanation: Normalising values to base MVA and kV simplifies grid studies across many voltage levels.

principles.name: Inrush current
principles.explanation: Closing a breaker at voltage zero can saturate core — generating harmonic-rich magnetising currents mistaken for faults.

components.name: HV and LV windings
components.function: Electromagnetic coupling; concentric or pancake layouts
components.madeFrom: Paper-insulated rectangular copper; transposed strands reduce eddy losses

components.name: Tank and conservator
components.criticalNote: Nitrogen blanket or bladder prevents moist air ingress

components.name: Bushings
components.function: Brings conductors through grounded tank at high voltage
components.madeFrom: Porcelain or composite with capacitive grading

rawMaterials.name: Kraft paper and cellulose
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Dry and oil impregnate under vacuum

rawMaterials.name: Transformer oil
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Natural esters for lower flash risk

buildSteps.title: Wind coils on mandrels
buildSteps.description: Maintain tension; add cooling ducts; transpose strands in large conductors.
buildSteps.warningNote: Sharp edges cut paper — radius tools

buildSteps.title: Stack core and insert coils
buildSteps.description: Interleave laminations; tighten core clamps; slide windings; install spacers.
buildSteps.warningNote: Foreign metal in window shorts turns

buildSteps.title: Vacuum oil impregnate
buildSteps.description: Heat and pull vacuum on windings; flood oil; soak; hold BDV test.

buildSteps.title: Tank weld and leak test
buildSteps.description: Pressure test with dry air; install bushings; fill conservator.
buildSteps.warningNote: Weld splatter inside tank is conductive debris

buildSteps.title: Energise and measure losses
buildSteps.description: Open-circuit and short-circuit tests extract equivalent circuit; compare to nameplate.
buildSteps.warningNote: Short-circuit test overheats small units quickly

history.event: Stanley and others commercialise practical AC transformers for distribution.
Three-phase demonstration in Lauffen–Frankfurt proves transformer-linked transmission.

inventors.name: Lucien Gaulard
inventors.contribution: Early transformer prototypes in AC distribution experiments

inventors.name: John Gibbs
inventors.contribution: Parallel AC transformer patent work with Gaulard

impact:
Transformers made long-distance AC grids economical — the enabling device between generator voltage and lightning-prone transmission lines.

They remain heavy, oil-filled monuments of electromagnetic craft.

videos.title: Transformer equivalent circuit
videos.description: Accredited labs on open-circuit and short-circuit tests.

externalLinks.title: EIA — transformers and grid equipment context
